accurate.why: All claims align with the bill text and accurate context (Section 366 of EPCA governs State energy security plans, administered by DOE).

no_hallucinations.why: No fabricated claims; naming GAO for Comptroller General and DOE/Secretary of Energy is accurate context consistent with the bill.

states_purpose.why: Clearly identifies the bill's purpose: amending EPCA Section 366 to include physical security, cybersecurity, and resilience of local distribution systems in State energy security plans.

changes_to_existing_law.why: Correctly describes the amendments to Section 366 of the Energy Policy and Conservation Act, including the new definition, consultation, content, sunset, and may-to-shall changes.

exceptions_conditions.why: Notes key conditions: submission required for eligibility but no Secretary approval needed, and the protected-information annex carve-out.

effective_dates.why: Correctly states the September 30, 2031 sunset and the September 30, 2030 GAO report deadline.

major_provisions_covered.why: Captures all essential operative provisions: definition, consultation expansion, content revisions, approval clarification, may-to-shall, sunset, and GAO report.

objective_tone.why: Neutral, descriptive tone with no advocacy or editorializing.

coherent.why: Well-organized and internally consistent, following the bill's structure clearly.

concise.why: Efficient summary without padding or repetition.

correct_entities.why: Correctly names EPCA, Section 366, GAO/Comptroller General, Secretary of Energy, and the two congressional committees.

correct_figures.why: All figures stated (100 kilovolts, September 30, 2031 sunset, September 30, 2030 report deadline) match the bill text.
